1→package com.secman.service
     2→
     3→import com.secman.domain.Asset
     4→import com.secman.domain.Vulnerability
     5→import com.secman.domain.VulnerabilityException
     6→import com.secman.domain.VulnerabilityException.ExceptionType
     7→import com.secman.dto.CreateVulnerabilityExceptionRequest
     8→import com.secman.dto.UpdateVulnerabilityExceptionRequest
     9→import com.secman.dto.VulnerabilityExceptionDto
    10→import com.secman.repository.AssetRepository
    11→import com.secman.repository.VulnerabilityExceptionRepository
    12→import com.secman.repository.VulnerabilityRepository
    13→import jakarta.inject.Singleton
    14→import org.slf4j.LoggerFactory
    15→import java.time.LocalDateTime
    16→
    17→/**
    18→ * Service for vulnerability exception management
    19→ *
    20→ * Handles:
    21→ * - Creating, updating, deleting exceptions (IP, PRODUCT, ASSET, CVE types)
    22→ * - Checking if vulnerabilities are excepted
    23→ * - Managing exception lifecycle (active/expired)
    24→ *
    25→ * Related to: Feature 004-i-want-to (VULN Role & Vulnerability Management UI)
    26→ * Related to: Feature 021-vulnerability-overdue-exception-logic (Added ASSET support)
    27→ */
    28→@Singleton
    29→class VulnerabilityExceptionService(
    30→    private val vulnerabilityExceptionRepository: VulnerabilityExceptionRepository,
    31→    private val assetRepository: AssetRepository,
    32→    private val vulnerabilityRepository: VulnerabilityRepository  // Feature 021 Phase 3: For affected counts
    33→) {
    34→    private val log = LoggerFactory.getLogger(VulnerabilityExceptionService::class.java)
    35→
    36→    /**
    37→     * Get all active exceptions (not expired)
    38→     *
    39→     * @return List of active vulnerability exceptions
    40→     */
    41→    fun getActiveExceptions(): List<VulnerabilityException> {
    42→        val now = LocalDateTime.now()
    43→        return vulnerabilityExceptionRepository.findByExpirationDateIsNullOrExpirationDateGreaterThan(now)
    44→    }
    45→
    46→    /**
    47→     * Check if a vulnerability is excepted
    48→     *
    49→     * @param vulnerability The vulnerability to check
    50→     * @param asset The asset the vulnerability is on
    51→     * @return True if vulnerability is excepted, false otherwise
    52→     */
    53→    fun isVulnerabilityExcepted(vulnerability: Vulnerability, asset: Asset): Boolean {
    54→        val activeExceptions = getActiveExceptions()
    55→
    56→        // Check if any exception matches this vulnerability
    57→        return activeExceptions.any { exception ->
    58→            exception.matches(vulnerability, asset)
    59→        }
    60→    }
    61→
    62→    /**
    63→     * Find the matching exception for a vulnerability
    64→     *
    65→     * @param vulnerability The vulnerability to check
    66→     * @param asset The asset the vulnerability is on
    67→     * @return The matching exception, or null if none found
    68→     */
    69→    fun findMatchingException(vulnerability: Vulnerability, asset: Asset): VulnerabilityException? {
    70→        val activeExceptions = getActiveExceptions()
    71→
    72→        // Return the first matching exception
    73→        return activeExceptions.firstOrNull { exception ->
    74→            exception.matches(vulnerability, asset)
    75→        }
    76→    }
    77→
    78→    /**
    79→     * Get all exceptions with optional filters
    80→     *
    81→     * Feature 021 Phase 3: Now includes affected vulnerability counts
    82→     *
    83→     * @param activeOnly If true, only return active exceptions
    84→     * @param type Optional filter by exception type
    85→     * @param includeAffectedCount If true, calculate affected vulnerability count (more expensive)
    86→     * @return List of exception DTOs
    87→     */
    88→    fun getAllExceptions(
    89→        activeOnly: Boolean = false,
    90→        type: ExceptionType? = null,
    91→        includeAffectedCount: Boolean = true  // Feature 021 Phase 3: Default true
    92→    ): List<VulnerabilityExceptionDto> {
    93→        log.debug("Getting all exceptions - activeOnly: {}, type: {}, includeAffectedCount: {}",
    94→            activeOnly, type, includeAffectedCount)
    95→
    96→        val exceptions = when {
    97→            activeOnly && type != null -> {
    98→                getActiveExceptions().filter { it.exceptionType == type }
    99→            }
   100→            activeOnly -> {
   101→                getActiveExceptions()
   102→            }
   103→            type != null -> {
   104→                vulnerabilityExceptionRepository.findByExceptionType(type)
   105→            }
   106→            else -> {
   107→                vulnerabilityExceptionRepository.findAll().toList()
   108→            }
   109→        }
   110→
   111→        // Map to DTOs with optional affected count
   112→        return exceptions.map { exception ->
   113→            mapToDto(exception, includeAffectedCount)
   114→        }
   115→    }
   116→
   117→    /**
   118→     * Create a new vulnerability exception
   119→     *
   120→     * Feature 021: Now supports ASSET type with validation
   121→     *
   122→     * @param request The exception creation request
   123→     * @param username The username of the user creating the exception
   124→     * @return The created exception DTO
   125→     * @throws IllegalArgumentException if ASSET exception with invalid assetId
   126→     */
   127→    fun createException(request: CreateVulnerabilityExceptionRequest, username: String): VulnerabilityExceptionDto {
   128→        log.info("Creating exception - type: {}, target: {}, assetId: {}, by: {}",
   129→            request.exceptionType, request.targetValue, request.assetId, username)
   130→
   131→        // Validate ASSET exception
   132→        if (request.exceptionType == ExceptionType.ASSET) {
   133→            if (request.assetId == null) {
   134→                throw IllegalArgumentException("Asset ID is required for ASSET-type exceptions")
   135→            }
   136→            // Verify asset exists
   137→            val asset = assetRepository.findById(request.assetId).orElse(null)
   138→                ?: throw IllegalArgumentException("Asset not found with id: ${request.assetId}")
   139→
   140→            log.debug("ASSET exception validated for asset: {} ({})", asset.name, asset.id)
   141→        }
   142→
   143→        // Validate CVE exception (supports comma-separated CVE IDs)
   144→        if (request.exceptionType == ExceptionType.CVE) {
   145→            val cvePattern = Regex("^CVE-\\d{4}-\\d+$", RegexOption.IGNORE_CASE)
   146→            val cveIds = request.targetValue.split(",").map { it.trim() }.filter { it.isNotEmpty() }
   147→            if (cveIds.isEmpty()) {
   148→                throw IllegalArgumentException("At least one CVE ID is required")
   149→            }
   150→            for (cveId in cveIds) {
   151→                if (!cveId.matches(cvePattern)) {
   152→                    throw IllegalArgumentException("Invalid CVE ID format: '$cveId'. Expected format: CVE-YYYY-NNNNN")
   153→                }
   154→            }
   155→            if (request.assetId != null) {
   156→                assetRepository.findById(request.assetId).orElse(null)
   157→                    ?: throw IllegalArgumentException("Asset not found with id: ${request.assetId}")
   158→            }
   159→        }
   160→
   161→        // Normalize CVE targetValue: strip spaces around commas for FIND_IN_SET compatibility
   162→        val normalizedTargetValue = if (request.exceptionType == ExceptionType.CVE) {
   163→            request.targetValue.split(",").map { it.trim() }.filter { it.isNotEmpty() }.joinToString(",")
   164→        } else {
   165→            request.targetValue.trim()
   166→        }
   167→
   168→        val exception = VulnerabilityException(
   169→            exceptionType = request.exceptionType,
   170→            targetValue = normalizedTargetValue,
   171→            assetId = request.assetId,  // Feature 021: Set asset ID
   172→            expirationDate = request.expirationDate,
   173→            reason = request.reason.trim(),
   174→            createdBy = username
   175→        )
   176→
   177→        val saved = vulnerabilityExceptionRepository.save(exception)
   178→        log.info("Created exception with id: {}", saved.id)
   179→
   180→        return mapToDto(saved, includeAffectedCount = false) // Don't calculate on create (no vulns yet potentially)
   181→    }
   182→
   183→    /**
   184→     * Update an existing vulnerability exception
   185→     *
   186→     * Feature 021: Now supports updating ASSET type with validation
   187→     *
   188→     * @param id The exception ID to update
   189→     * @param request The exception update request
   190→     * @return The updated exception DTO
   191→     * @throws IllegalArgumentException if exception not found or invalid asset ID
   192→     */
   193→    fun updateException(id: Long, request: UpdateVulnerabilityExceptionRequest): VulnerabilityExceptionDto {
   194→        log.info("Updating exception id: {}", id)
   195→
   196→        val exception = vulnerabilityExceptionRepository.findById(id).orElse(null)
   197→            ?: throw IllegalArgumentException("Vulnerability exception not found with id: $id")
   198→
   199→        // Validate ASSET exception
   200→        if (request.exceptionType == ExceptionType.ASSET) {
   201→            if (request.assetId == null) {
   202→                throw IllegalArgumentException("Asset ID is required for ASSET-type exceptions")
   203→            }
   204→            // Verify asset exists
   205→            val asset = assetRepository.findById(request.assetId).orElse(null)
   206→                ?: throw IllegalArgumentException("Asset not found with id: ${request.assetId}")
   207→
   208→            log.debug("ASSET exception validated for asset: {} ({})", asset.name, asset.id)
   209→        }
   210→
   211→        // Validate CVE exception (supports comma-separated CVE IDs)
   212→        if (request.exceptionType == ExceptionType.CVE) {
   213→            val cvePattern = Regex("^CVE-\\d{4}-\\d+$", RegexOption.IGNORE_CASE)
   214→            val cveIds = request.targetValue.split(",").map { it.trim() }.filter { it.isNotEmpty() }
   215→            if (cveIds.isEmpty()) {
   216→                throw IllegalArgumentException("At least one CVE ID is required")
   217→            }
   218→            for (cveId in cveIds) {
   219→                if (!cveId.matches(cvePattern)) {
   220→                    throw IllegalArgumentException("Invalid CVE ID format: '$cveId'. Expected format: CVE-YYYY-NNNNN")
   221→                }
   222→            }
   223→            if (request.assetId != null) {
   224→                assetRepository.findById(request.assetId).orElse(null)
   225→                    ?: throw IllegalArgumentException("Asset not found with id: ${request.assetId}")
   226→            }
   227→        }
   228→
   229→        exception.exceptionType = request.exceptionType
   230→        // Normalize CVE targetValue: strip spaces around commas for FIND_IN_SET compatibility
   231→        exception.targetValue = if (request.exceptionType == ExceptionType.CVE) {
   232→            request.targetValue.split(",").map { it.trim() }.filter { it.isNotEmpty() }.joinToString(",")
   233→        } else {
   234→            request.targetValue.trim()
   235→        }
   236→        exception.assetId = request.assetId  // Feature 021: Update asset ID
   237→        exception.expirationDate = request.expirationDate
   238→        exception.reason = request.reason.trim()
   239→
   240→        val updated = vulnerabilityExceptionRepository.update(exception)
   241→        log.info("Updated exception id: {}", id)
   242→
   243→        return mapToDto(updated, includeAffectedCount = false) // Don't calculate on update
   244→    }
   245→
   246→    /**
   247→     * Delete a vulnerability exception
   248→     *
   249→     * @param id The exception ID to delete
   250→     * @throws IllegalArgumentException if exception not found
   251→     */
   252→    fun deleteException(id: Long) {
   253→        log.info("Deleting exception id: {}", id)
   254→
   255→        val exception = vulnerabilityExceptionRepository.findById(id).orElse(null)
   256→            ?: throw IllegalArgumentException("Vulnerability exception not found with id: $id")
   257→
   258→        vulnerabilityExceptionRepository.deleteById(id)
   259→        log.info("Deleted exception id: {}", id)
   260→    }
   261→
   262→    /**
   263→     * Calculate how many vulnerabilities are affected by an exception
   264→     *
   265→     * Feature 021 Phase 3: Exception impact calculation
   266→     *
   267→     * @param exception The exception to check
   268→     * @return Count of affected vulnerabilities
   269→     */
   270→    fun calculateAffectedCount(exception: VulnerabilityException): Int {
   271→        return when (exception.exceptionType) {
   272→            ExceptionType.IP -> {
   273→                try {
   274→                    vulnerabilityRepository.countByAssetIp(exception.targetValue).toInt()
   275→                } catch (e: Exception) {
   276→                    log.warn("Failed to count IP-based exceptions: {}", e.message)
   277→                    0
   278→                }
   279→            }
   280→            ExceptionType.PRODUCT -> {
   281→                // Count vulnerabilities matching product pattern
   282→                try {
   283→                    vulnerabilityRepository.countByVulnerableProductVersionsContainingIgnoreCase(
   284→                        exception.targetValue
   285→                    ).toInt()
   286→                } catch (e: Exception) {
   287→                    log.warn("Failed to count product-based exceptions: {}", e.message)
   288→                    0
   289→                }
   290→            }
   291→            ExceptionType.ASSET -> {
   292→                // Count vulnerabilities for this specific asset
   293→                if (exception.assetId != null) {
   294→                    try {
   295→                        vulnerabilityRepository.countByAssetId(exception.assetId!!).toInt()
   296→                    } catch (e: Exception) {
   297→                        log.warn("Failed to count asset-based exceptions: {}", e.message)
   298→                        0
   299→                    }
   300→                } else {
   301→                    0
   302→                }
   303→            }
   304→            ExceptionType.CVE -> {
   305→                try {
   306→                    val cveIds = exception.targetValue.split(",").map { it.trim() }.filter { it.isNotEmpty() }
   307→                    if (exception.assetId != null) {
   308→                        vulnerabilityRepository.countByVulnerabilityIdInAndAssetId(cveIds, exception.assetId!!).toInt()
   309→                    } else {
   310→                        vulnerabilityRepository.countByVulnerabilityIdIn(cveIds).toInt()
   311→                    }
   312→                } catch (e: Exception) {
   313→                    log.warn("Failed to count CVE-based exceptions: {}", e.message)
   314→                    0
   315→                }
   316→            }
   317→        }
   318→    }
   319→
   320→    /**
   321→     * Map VulnerabilityException entity to DTO
   322→     *
   323→     * Feature 021: Now includes assetId, assetName, and optionally affectedCount
   324→     * Feature 021 Phase 3: Added affected count calculation
   325→     *
   326→     * @param exception The exception entity
   327→     * @param includeAffectedCount If true, calculate affected vulnerability count
   328→     * @return Exception DTO
   329→     */
   330→    private fun mapToDto(
   331→        exception: VulnerabilityException,
   332→        includeAffectedCount: Boolean = false
   333→    ): VulnerabilityExceptionDto {
   334→        // Fetch asset name if this is an ASSET or CVE exception with assetId
   335→        val assetName = if ((exception.exceptionType == ExceptionType.ASSET || exception.exceptionType == ExceptionType.CVE)
   336→            && exception.assetId != null) {
   337→            assetRepository.findById(exception.assetId!!).orElse(null)?.name
   338→        } else {
   339→            null
   340→        }
   341→
   342→        // Calculate affected count if requested
   343→        val affectedCount = if (includeAffectedCount) {
   344→            calculateAffectedCount(exception)
   345→        } else {
   346→            null
   347→        }
   348→        
   349→        return VulnerabilityExceptionDto(
   350→            id = exception.id,
   351→            exceptionType = exception.exceptionType,
   352→            targetValue = exception.targetValue,
   353→            expirationDate = exception.expirationDate,
   354→            reason = exception.reason,
   355→            createdBy = exception.createdBy,
   356→            createdAt = exception.createdAt,
   357→            updatedAt = exception.updatedAt,
   358→            isActive = exception.isActive(),
   359→            affectedVulnerabilityCount = affectedCount,  // Feature 021 Phase 3
   360→            assetId = exception.assetId,  // Feature 021
   361→            assetName = assetName  // Feature 021
   362→        )
   363→    }
   364→}
   365→

content.text:
I need to understand how vulnerability exceptions are deleted and what happens to their associated requests.

Please explore:

1. Find VulnerabilityExceptionService or VulnerabilityExceptionController - look for delete/cancel methods
2. Find VulnerabilityExceptionRepository - look for any delete methods
3. Find the domain entities: VulnerabilityException and VulnerabilityExceptionRequest - understand their relationship and status fields
4. Look at VulnerabilityExceptionsTable.tsx and ExceptionApprovalDashboard.tsx in the frontend for how admin deletes exceptions
5. Check the vulnerability exception request DTO for status fields

The key question: When an exception is deleted, does the system update the status of the original request that created it? If the request stays as "APPROVED" even after the exception is deleted, that would cause the duplicate check to incorrectly block new requests.

Search in:
- src/backendng/src/main/kotlin/com/secman/domain/
- src/backendng/src/main/kotlin/com/secman/service/
- src/backendng/src/main/kotlin/com/secman/controller/
- src/backendng/src/main/kotlin/com/secman/repository/
- src/frontend/src/components/VulnerabilityExceptionsTable.tsx
- src/frontend/src/components/ExceptionApprovalDashboard.tsx

Read full files to understand the complete picture.

   108→    /**

1→package com.secman.domain
     2→
     3→import io.micronaut.serde.annotation.Serdeable
     4→import jakarta.persistence.*
     5→import jakarta.validation.constraints.NotBlank
     6→import jakarta.validation.constraints.NotNull
     7→import jakarta.validation.constraints.Size
     8→import java.time.LocalDateTime
     9→
    10→/**
    11→ * VulnerabilityException entity representing an exception rule for vulnerabilities.
    12→ *
    13→ * Exceptions can be:
    14→ * - IP-based: Applies to all vulnerabilities on a specific IP address
    15→ * - PRODUCT-based: Applies to all vulnerabilities matching a product pattern
    16→ * - ASSET-based: Applies to all vulnerabilities on a specific asset (by ID)
    17→ * - CVE-based: Applies to a specific CVE ID, optionally scoped to one asset
    18→ *   - targetValue = one or more comma-separated CVE IDs (e.g., "CVE-2023-29326,CVE-2024-1234")
    19→ *   - assetId = null for system-wide, or asset ID for single-system scope
    20→ *
    21→ * All exceptions require an expiration date (Feature 021).
    22→ * Time-limited exceptions enforce periodic review.
    23→ *
    24→ * Related to: Feature 004-i-want-to (VULN Role & Vulnerability Management UI)
    25→ * Related to: Feature 021-vulnerability-overdue-exception-logic (Asset exceptions)
    26→ */
    27→@Entity
    28→@Table(
    29→    name = "vulnerability_exception",
    30→    indexes = [
    31→        Index(name = "idx_vuln_exception_type", columnList = "exception_type"),
    32→        Index(name = "idx_vuln_exception_expiration", columnList = "expiration_date"),
    33→        Index(name = "idx_vuln_exception_asset", columnList = "asset_id"),
    34→        // PERFORMANCE: Composite indexes for faster exception matching queries
    35→        Index(name = "idx_vuln_exception_type_target", columnList = "exception_type, target_value"),
    36→        Index(name = "idx_vuln_exception_active", columnList = "expiration_date, exception_type")
    37→    ]
    38→)
    39→@Serdeable
    40→data class VulnerabilityException(
    41→    @Id
    42→    @GeneratedValue(strategy = GenerationType.IDENTITY)
    43→    var id: Long? = null,
    44→
    45→    /**
    46→     * Type of exception: IP, PRODUCT, ASSET, or CVE
    47→     */
    48→    @Enumerated(EnumType.STRING)
    49→    @Column(name = "exception_type", nullable = false, length = 20)
    50→    @NotNull
    51→    var exceptionType: ExceptionType,
    52→
    53→    /**
    54→     * Target value for the exception:
    55→     * - For IP: exact IP address (e.g., "192.168.1.10")
    56→     * - For PRODUCT: product name/version pattern (e.g., "OpenSSH 7.4")
    57→     * - For ASSET: not used (assetId field used instead)
    58→     * - For CVE: one or more comma-separated CVE identifiers (e.g., "CVE-2023-29326,CVE-2024-1234")
    59→     */
    60→    @Column(name = "target_value", nullable = false, length = 512)
    61→    @NotBlank
    62→    @Size(max = 512)
    63→    var targetValue: String,
    64→
    65→    /**
    66→     * Asset ID for ASSET-type and CVE-type exceptions
    67→     * - For IP/PRODUCT exceptions: null
    68→     * - For ASSET exceptions: ID of the specific asset
    69→     * - For CVE exceptions: null (system-wide) or asset ID (single-system scope)
    70→     */
    71→    @Column(name = "asset_id")
    72→    var assetId: Long? = null,
    73→
    74→    /**
    75→     * Expiration date for the exception
    76→     * Feature 021: All exceptions now require an end date
    77→     * - null = legacy exception (from before Feature 021)
    78→     * - non-null = exception expires after this date (required going forward)
    79→     */
    80→    @Column(name = "expiration_date")
    81→    var expirationDate: LocalDateTime? = null,
    82→
    83→    /**
    84→     * Reason/justification for the exception
    85→     */
    86→    @Column(name = "reason", nullable = false, length = 1024)
    87→    @NotBlank
    88→    @Size(max = 1024)
    89→    var reason: String,
    90→
    91→    /**
    92→     * User who created the exception
    93→     */
    94→    @Column(name = "created_by", nullable = false, length = 255)
    95→    @NotBlank
    96→    @Size(max = 255)
    97→    var createdBy: String,
    98→
    99→    @Column(name = "created_at", updatable = false)
   100→    var createdAt: LocalDateTime? = null,
   101→
   102→    @Column(name = "updated_at")
   103→    var updatedAt: LocalDateTime? = null
   104→) {
   105→    enum class ExceptionType {
   106→        IP,      // Exception applies to specific IP address
   107→        PRODUCT, // Exception applies to product name/version pattern
   108→        ASSET,   // Exception applies to specific asset by ID (Feature 021)
   109→        CVE      // Exception applies to specific CVE ID, optionally scoped to one asset
   110→    }
   111→
   112→    @PrePersist
   113→    fun onCreate() {
   114→        val now = LocalDateTime.now()
   115→        createdAt = now
   116→        updatedAt = now
   117→    }
   118→
   119→    @PreUpdate
   120→    fun onUpdate() {
   121→        updatedAt = LocalDateTime.now()
   122→    }
   123→
   124→    /**
   125→     * Check if this exception is currently active
   126→     */
   127→    fun isActive(): Boolean {
   128→        return expirationDate == null || expirationDate!!.isAfter(LocalDateTime.now())
   129→    }
   130→
   131→    /**
   132→     * Check if this exception matches the given vulnerability and asset
   133→     *
   134→     * Feature 021: Added ASSET type matching by asset ID
   135→     * Bug fix: PRODUCT type now also matches CVE ID patterns
   136→     */
   137→    fun matches(vulnerability: Vulnerability, asset: Asset): Boolean {
   138→        if (!isActive()) return false
   139→
   140→        return when (exceptionType) {
   141→            ExceptionType.IP -> asset.ip == targetValue
   142→            ExceptionType.PRODUCT -> {
   143→                // Match either CVE ID (e.g., "CVE-2023-29326") or product version string
   144→                vulnerability.vulnerabilityId == targetValue ||
   145→                vulnerability.vulnerableProductVersions?.contains(targetValue) == true
   146→            }
   147→            ExceptionType.ASSET -> assetId != null && assetId == asset.id
   148→            ExceptionType.CVE -> {
   149→                val cveIds = targetValue.split(",").map { it.trim() }
   150→                val cveMatches = vulnerability.vulnerabilityId in cveIds
   151→                if (assetId != null) {
   152→                    cveMatches && assetId == asset.id
   153→                } else {
   154→                    cveMatches
   155→                }
   156→            }
   157→        }
   158→    }
   159→
   160→    override fun toString(): String {
   161→        return "VulnerabilityException(id=$id, type=$exceptionType, target='$targetValue', " +
   162→                "assetId=$assetId, expires=${expirationDate ?: "never"}, active=${isActive()})"
   163→    }
   164→
   165→    override fun equals(other: Any?): Boolean {
   166→        if (this === other) return true
   167→        if (other !is VulnerabilityException) return false
   168→        return id != null && id == other.id
   169→    }
   170→
   171→    override fun hashCode(): Int {
   172→        return id?.hashCode() ?: 0
   173→    }
   174→}
   175→

1→package com.secman.domain
     2→
     3→import com.fasterxml.jackson.annotation.JsonIgnore
     4→import io.micronaut.serde.annotation.Serdeable
     5→import jakarta.persistence.*
     6→import jakarta.validation.constraints.NotBlank
     7→import jakarta.validation.constraints.NotNull
     8→import jakarta.validation.constraints.Size
     9→import org.hibernate.annotations.OnDelete
    10→import org.hibernate.annotations.OnDeleteAction
    11→import java.time.LocalDateTime
    12→
    13→/**
    14→ * VulnerabilityExceptionRequest entity representing a request to create an exception for a vulnerability.
    15→ *
    16→ * Workflow:
    17→ * - Regular users create requests with PENDING status (requires approval)
    18→ * - ADMIN/SECCHAMPION users get auto-approved requests (status immediately APPROVED)
    19→ * - ADMIN/SECCHAMPION can approve or reject PENDING requests
    20→ * - Approved requests create corresponding VulnerabilityException entries
    21→ *
    22→ * State machine:
    23→ * - PENDING → APPROVED, REJECTED, CANCELLED
    24→ * - APPROVED → EXPIRED
    25→ * - REJECTED, CANCELLED, EXPIRED → (terminal states)
    26→ *
    27→ * Related to: Feature 031-vuln-exception-approval (Vulnerability Exception Request & Approval Workflow)
    28→ *
    29→ * @property id Unique identifier
    30→ * @property vulnerability Vulnerability this request applies to
    31→ * @property requestedByUser User who submitted the request (nullable for audit after user deletion)
    32→ * @property requestedByUsername Username of requester (preserved for audit trail)
    33→ * @property scope Exception scope (SINGLE_VULNERABILITY or CVE_PATTERN)
    34→ * @property reason Business justification (50-2048 characters)
    35→ * @property expirationDate When the exception should expire
    36→ * @property status Current status (PENDING, APPROVED, REJECTED, EXPIRED, CANCELLED)
    37→ * @property autoApproved True if ADMIN/SECCHAMPION auto-approved
    38→ * @property reviewedByUser Reviewer who approved/rejected (nullable for audit after user deletion)
    39→ * @property reviewedByUsername Username of reviewer (preserved for audit trail)
    40→ * @property reviewDate When the request was reviewed
    41→ * @property reviewComment Reviewer's notes/justification
    42→ * @property createdAt When the request was created
    43→ * @property updatedAt When the request was last modified
    44→ * @property version Optimistic locking version for concurrency control
    45→ */
    46→@Entity
    47→@Table(
    48→    name = "vulnerability_exception_request",
    49→    indexes = [
    50→        Index(name = "idx_vuln_req_vulnerability", columnList = "vulnerability_id"),
    51→        Index(name = "idx_vuln_req_status", columnList = "status"),
    52→        Index(name = "idx_vuln_req_requester", columnList = "requested_by_user_id"),
    53→        Index(name = "idx_vuln_req_reviewer", columnList = "reviewed_by_user_id"),
    54→        Index(name = "idx_vuln_req_created", columnList = "created_at"),
    55→        Index(name = "idx_vuln_req_expiration", columnList = "expiration_date")
    56→    ]
    57→)
    58→@Serdeable
    59→data class VulnerabilityExceptionRequest(
    60→    @Id
    61→    @GeneratedValue(strategy = GenerationType.IDENTITY)
    62→    var id: Long? = null,
    63→
    64→    /**
    65→     * Vulnerability this request applies to
    66→     * ManyToOne relationship with nullable for orphaned requests (vulnerability deleted)
    67→     *
    68→     * FK constraint: ON DELETE SET NULL - Preserves exception requests for audit trail
    69→     * when vulnerability is deleted (e.g., via asset cascade deletion)
    70→     */
    71→    @ManyToOne(fetch = FetchType.LAZY)
    72→    @JoinColumn(name = "vulnerability_id", nullable = true)
    73→    @OnDelete(action = OnDeleteAction.SET_NULL)
    74→    @JsonIgnore // Prevent lazy loading errors during JSON serialization
    75→    var vulnerability: Vulnerability? = null,
    76→
    77→    /**
    78→     * User who requested the exception
    79→     * Nullable: If user deleted, preserve username for audit trail
    80→     */
    81→    @ManyToOne(fetch = FetchType.LAZY)
    82→    @JoinColumn(name = "requested_by_user_id")
    83→    @JsonIgnore
    84→    var requestedByUser: User? = null,
    85→
    86→    /**
    87→     * Username of requester (denormalized for audit trail)
    88→     * Preserved even if user account deleted
    89→     */
    90→    @Column(name = "requested_by_username", nullable = false, length = 255)
    91→    @NotBlank
    92→    @Size(max = 255)
    93→    var requestedByUsername: String,
    94→
    95→    /**
    96→     * Exception scope: SINGLE_VULNERABILITY or CVE_PATTERN
    97→     */
    98→    @Enumerated(EnumType.STRING)
    99→    @Column(name = "scope", nullable = false, length = 20)
   100→    @NotNull
   101→    var scope: ExceptionScope,
   102→
   103→    /**
   104→     * Business justification for the exception
   105→     * Required: 50-2048 characters, plain text only (XSS sanitized)
   106→     */
   107→    @Column(name = "reason", nullable = false, length = 2048)
   108→    @NotBlank
   109→    @Size(min = 50, max = 2048)
   110→    var reason: String,
   111→
   112→    /**
   113→     * When the exception should expire
   114→     * Must be future date at creation
   115→     */
   116→    @Column(name = "expiration_date", nullable = false)
   117→    @NotNull
   118→    var expirationDate: LocalDateTime,
   119→
   120→    /**
   121→     * Current status: PENDING, APPROVED, REJECTED, EXPIRED, CANCELLED
   122→     */
   123→    @Enumerated(EnumType.STRING)
   124→    @Column(name = "status", nullable = false, length = 20)
   125→    @NotNull
   126→    var status: ExceptionRequestStatus,
   127→
   128→    /**
   129→     * True if this was auto-approved (requester has ADMIN/SECCHAMPION role)
   130→     */
   131→    @Column(name = "auto_approved", nullable = false)
   132→    var autoApproved: Boolean = false,
   133→
   134→    /**
   135→     * User who reviewed (approved or rejected) the request
   136→     * Nullable: If reviewer deleted, preserve username for audit trail
   137→     */
   138→    @ManyToOne(fetch = FetchType.LAZY)
   139→    @JoinColumn(name = "reviewed_by_user_id")
   140→    @JsonIgnore
   141→    var reviewedByUser: User? = null,
   142→
   143→    /**
   144→     * Username of reviewer (denormalized for audit trail)
   145→     * Preserved even if reviewer account deleted
   146→     */
   147→    @Column(name = "reviewed_by_username", length = 255)
   148→    @Size(max = 255)
   149→    var reviewedByUsername: String? = null,
   150→
   151→    /**
   152→     * When the request was reviewed (approved or rejected)
   153→     */
   154→    @Column(name = "review_date")
   155→    var reviewDate: LocalDateTime? = null,
   156→
   157→    /**
   158→     * Reviewer's comment/justification
   159→     * Optional for approval, required for rejection (10-1024 characters)
   160→     */
   161→    @Column(name = "review_comment", length = 1024)
   162→    @Size(max = 1024)
   163→    var reviewComment: String? = null,
   164→
   165→    @Column(name = "created_at", updatable = false)
   166→    var createdAt: LocalDateTime? = null,
   167→
   168→    @Column(name = "updated_at")
   169→    var updatedAt: LocalDateTime? = null,
   170→
   171→    /**
   172→     * Optimistic locking version for concurrency control
   173→     * Prevents race conditions when multiple users try to approve/reject simultaneously
   174→     */
   175→    @Version
   176→    @Column(name = "version", nullable = false)
   177→    var version: Long = 0
   178→) {
   179→    @PrePersist
   180→    fun onCreate() {
   181→        val now = LocalDateTime.now()
   182→        createdAt = now
   183→        updatedAt = now
   184→    }
   185→
   186→    @PreUpdate
   187→    fun onUpdate() {
   188→        updatedAt = LocalDateTime.now()
   189→    }
   190→
   191→    /**
   192→     * Check if this request can transition to the given status
   193→     */
   194→    fun canTransitionTo(newStatus: ExceptionRequestStatus): Boolean {
   195→        return status.canTransitionTo(newStatus)
   196→    }
   197→
   198→    /**
   199→     * Check if this request is in a terminal state (cannot be modified)
   200→     */
   201→    fun isTerminal(): Boolean {
   202→        return status.isTerminal()
   203→    }
   204→
   205→    override fun toString(): String {
   206→        return "VulnerabilityExceptionRequest(id=$id, status=$status, " +
   207→                "scope=$scope, requestedBy='$requestedByUsername', " +
   208→                "autoApproved=$autoApproved, reviewedBy='$reviewedByUsername', " +
   209→                "created=$createdAt)"
   210→    }
   211→
   212→    override fun equals(other: Any?): Boolean {
   213→        if (this === other) return true
   214→        if (other !is VulnerabilityExceptionRequest) return false
   215→        return id != null && id == other.id
   216→    }
   217→
   218→    override fun hashCode(): Int {
   219→        return id?.hashCode() ?: 0
   220→    }
   221→}
   222→

1→package com.secman.service
     2→
     3→import com.secman.domain.Asset
     4→import com.secman.domain.Vulnerability
     5→import com.secman.domain.VulnerabilityException
     6→import com.secman.domain.VulnerabilityException.ExceptionType
     7→import com.secman.dto.CreateVulnerabilityExceptionRequest
     8→import com.secman.dto.UpdateVulnerabilityExceptionRequest
     9→import com.secman.dto.VulnerabilityExceptionDto
    10→import com.secman.repository.AssetRepository
    11→import com.secman.repository.VulnerabilityExceptionRepository
    12→import com.secman.repository.VulnerabilityRepository
    13→import jakarta.inject.Singleton
    14→import org.slf4j.LoggerFactory
    15→import java.time.LocalDateTime
    16→
    17→/**
    18→ * Service for vulnerability exception management
    19→ *
    20→ * Handles:
    21→ * - Creating, updating, deleting exceptions (IP, PRODUCT, ASSET, CVE types)
    22→ * - Checking if vulnerabilities are excepted
    23→ * - Managing exception lifecycle (active/expired)
    24→ *
    25→ * Related to: Feature 004-i-want-to (VULN Role & Vulnerability Management UI)
    26→ * Related to: Feature 021-vulnerability-overdue-exception-logic (Added ASSET support)
    27→ */
    28→@Singleton
    29→class VulnerabilityExceptionService(
    30→    private val vulnerabilityExceptionRepository: VulnerabilityExceptionRepository,
    31→    private val assetRepository: AssetRepository,
    32→    private val vulnerabilityRepository: VulnerabilityRepository  // Feature 021 Phase 3: For affected counts
    33→) {
    34→    private val log = LoggerFactory.getLogger(VulnerabilityExceptionService::class.java)
    35→
    36→    /**
    37→     * Get all active exceptions (not expired)
    38→     *
    39→     * @return List of active vulnerability exceptions
    40→     */
    41→    fun getActiveExceptions(): List<VulnerabilityException> {
    42→        val now = LocalDateTime.now()
    43→        return vulnerabilityExceptionRepository.findByExpirationDateIsNullOrExpirationDateGreaterThan(now)
    44→    }
    45→
    46→    /**
    47→     * Check if a vulnerability is excepted
    48→     *
    49→     * @param vulnerability The vulnerability to check
    50→     * @param asset The asset the vulnerability is on
    51→     * @return True if vulnerability is excepted, false otherwise
    52→     */
    53→    fun isVulnerabilityExcepted(vulnerability: Vulnerability, asset: Asset): Boolean {
    54→        val activeExceptions = getActiveExceptions()
    55→
    56→        // Check if any exception matches this vulnerability
    57→        return activeExceptions.any { exception ->
    58→            exception.matches(vulnerability, asset)
    59→        }
    60→    }
    61→
    62→    /**
    63→     * Find the matching exception for a vulnerability
    64→     *
    65→     * @param vulnerability The vulnerability to check
    66→     * @param asset The asset the vulnerability is on
    67→     * @return The matching exception, or null if none found
    68→     */
    69→    fun findMatchingException(vulnerability: Vulnerability, asset: Asset): VulnerabilityException? {
    70→        val activeExceptions = getActiveExceptions()
    71→
    72→        // Return the first matching exception
    73→        return activeExceptions.firstOrNull { exception ->
    74→            exception.matches(vulnerability, asset)
    75→        }
    76→    }
    77→
    78→    /**
    79→     * Get all exceptions with optional filters
    80→     *
    81→     * Feature 021 Phase 3: Now includes affected vulnerability counts
    82→     *
    83→     * @param activeOnly If true, only return active exceptions
    84→     * @param type Optional filter by exception type
    85→     * @param includeAffectedCount If true, calculate affected vulnerability count (more expensive)
    86→     * @return List of exception DTOs
    87→     */
    88→    fun getAllExceptions(
    89→        activeOnly: Boolean = false,
    90→        type: ExceptionType? = null,
    91→        includeAffectedCount: Boolean = true  // Feature 021 Phase 3: Default true
    92→    ): List<VulnerabilityExceptionDto> {
    93→        log.debug("Getting all exceptions - activeOnly: {}, type: {}, includeAffectedCount: {}",
    94→            activeOnly, type, includeAffectedCount)
    95→
    96→        val exceptions = when {
    97→            activeOnly && type != null -> {
    98→                getActiveExceptions().filter { it.exceptionType == type }
    99→            }
   100→            activeOnly -> {
   101→                getActiveExceptions()
   102→            }
   103→            type != null -> {
   104→                vulnerabilityExceptionRepository.findByExceptionType(type)
   105→            }
   106→            else -> {
   107→                vulnerabilityExceptionRepository.findAll().toList()
   108→            }
   109→        }
   110→
   111→        // Map to DTOs with optional affected count
   112→        return exceptions.map { exception ->
   113→            mapToDto(exception, includeAffectedCount)
   114→        }
   115→    }
   116→
   117→    /**
   118→     * Create a new vulnerability exception
   119→     *
   120→     * Feature 021: Now supports ASSET type with validation
   121→     *
   122→     * @param request The exception creation request
   123→     * @param username The username of the user creating the exception
   124→     * @return The created exception DTO
   125→     * @throws IllegalArgumentException if ASSET exception with invalid assetId
   126→     */
   127→    fun createException(request: CreateVulnerabilityExceptionRequest, username: String): VulnerabilityExceptionDto {
   128→        log.info("Creating exception - type: {}, target: {}, assetId: {}, by: {}",
   129→            request.exceptionType, request.targetValue, request.assetId, username)
   130→
   131→        // Validate ASSET exception
   132→        if (request.exceptionType == ExceptionType.ASSET) {
   133→            if (request.assetId == null) {
   134→                throw IllegalArgumentException("Asset ID is required for ASSET-type exceptions")
   135→            }
   136→            // Verify asset exists
   137→            val asset = assetRepository.findById(request.assetId).orElse(null)
   138→                ?: throw IllegalArgumentException("Asset not found with id: ${request.assetId}")
   139→
   140→            log.debug("ASSET exception validated for asset: {} ({})", asset.name, asset.id)
   141→        }
   142→
   143→        // Validate CVE exception (supports comma-separated CVE IDs)
   144→        if (request.exceptionType == ExceptionType.CVE) {
   145→            val cvePattern = Regex("^CVE-\\d{4}-\\d+$", RegexOption.IGNORE_CASE)
   146→            val cveIds = request.targetValue.split(",").map { it.trim() }.filter { it.isNotEmpty() }
   147→            if (cveIds.isEmpty()) {
   148→                throw IllegalArgumentException("At least one CVE ID is required")
   149→            }
   150→            for (cveId in cveIds) {
   151→                if (!cveId.matches(cvePattern)) {
   152→                    throw IllegalArgumentException("Invalid CVE ID format: '$cveId'. Expected format: CVE-YYYY-NNNNN")
   153→                }
   154→            }
   155→            if (request.assetId != null) {
   156→                assetRepository.findById(request.assetId).orElse(null)
   157→                    ?: throw IllegalArgumentException("Asset not found with id: ${request.assetId}")
   158→            }
   159→        }
   160→
   161→        // Normalize CVE targetValue: strip spaces around commas for FIND_IN_SET compatibility
   162→        val normalizedTargetValue = if (request.exceptionType == ExceptionType.CVE) {
   163→            request.targetValue.split(",").map { it.trim() }.filter { it.isNotEmpty() }.joinToString(",")
   164→        } else {
   165→            request.targetValue.trim()
   166→        }
   167→
   168→        val exception = VulnerabilityException(
   169→            exceptionType = request.exceptionType,
   170→            targetValue = normalizedTargetValue,
   171→            assetId = request.assetId,  // Feature 021: Set asset ID
   172→            expirationDate = request.expirationDate,
   173→            reason = request.reason.trim(),
   174→            createdBy = username
   175→        )
   176→
   177→        val saved = vulnerabilityExceptionRepository.save(exception)
   178→        log.info("Created exception with id: {}", saved.id)
   179→
   180→        return mapToDto(saved, includeAffectedCount = false) // Don't calculate on create (no vulns yet potentially)
   181→    }
   182→
   183→    /**
   184→     * Update an existing vulnerability exception
   185→     *
   186→     * Feature 021: Now supports updating ASSET type with validation
   187→     *
   188→     * @param id The exception ID to update
   189→     * @param request The exception update request
   190→     * @return The updated exception DTO
   191→     * @throws IllegalArgumentException if exception not found or invalid asset ID
   192→     */
   193→    fun updateException(id: Long, request: UpdateVulnerabilityExceptionRequest): VulnerabilityExceptionDto {
   194→        log.info("Updating exception id: {}", id)
   195→
   196→        val exception = vulnerabilityExceptionRepository.findById(id).orElse(null)
   197→            ?: throw IllegalArgumentException("Vulnerability exception not found with id: $id")
   198→
   199→        // Validate ASSET exception
   200→        if (request.exceptionType == ExceptionType.ASSET) {
   201→            if (request.assetId == null) {
   202→                throw IllegalArgumentException("Asset ID is required for ASSET-type exceptions")
   203→            }
   204→            // Verify asset exists
   205→            val asset = assetRepository.findById(request.assetId).orElse(null)
   206→                ?: throw IllegalArgumentException("Asset not found with id: ${request.assetId}")
   207→
   208→            log.debug("ASSET exception validated for asset: {} ({})", asset.name, asset.id)
   209→        }
   210→
   211→        // Validate CVE exception (supports comma-separated CVE IDs)
   212→        if (request.exceptionType == ExceptionType.CVE) {
   213→            val cvePattern = Regex("^CVE-\\d{4}-\\d+$", RegexOption.IGNORE_CASE)
   214→            val cveIds = request.targetValue.split(",").map { it.trim() }.filter { it.isNotEmpty() }
   215→            if (cveIds.isEmpty()) {
   216→                throw IllegalArgumentException("At least one CVE ID is required")
   217→            }
   218→            for (cveId in cveIds) {
   219→                if (!cveId.matches(cvePattern)) {
   220→                    throw IllegalArgumentException("Invalid CVE ID format: '$cveId'. Expected format: CVE-YYYY-NNNNN")
   221→                }
   222→            }
   223→            if (request.assetId != null) {
   224→                assetRepository.findById(request.assetId).orElse(null)
   225→                    ?: throw IllegalArgumentException("Asset not found with id: ${request.assetId}")
   226→            }
   227→        }
   228→
   229→        exception.exceptionType = request.exceptionType
   230→        // Normalize CVE targetValue: strip spaces around commas for FIND_IN_SET compatibility
   231→        exception.targetValue = if (request.exceptionType == ExceptionType.CVE) {
   232→            request.targetValue.split(",").map { it.trim() }.filter { it.isNotEmpty() }.joinToString(",")
   233→        } else {
   234→            request.targetValue.trim()
   235→        }
   236→        exception.assetId = request.assetId  // Feature 021: Update asset ID
   237→        exception.expirationDate = request.expirationDate
   238→        exception.reason = request.reason.trim()
   239→
   240→        val updated = vulnerabilityExceptionRepository.update(exception)
   241→        log.info("Updated exception id: {}", id)
   242→
   243→        return mapToDto(updated, includeAffectedCount = false) // Don't calculate on update
   244→    }
   245→
   246→    /**
   247→     * Delete a vulnerability exception
   248→     *
   249→     * @param id The exception ID to delete
   250→     * @throws IllegalArgumentException if exception not found
   251→     */
   252→    fun deleteException(id: Long) {
   253→        log.info("Deleting exception id: {}", id)
   254→
   255→        val exception = vulnerabilityExceptionRepository.findById(id).orElse(null)
   256→            ?: throw IllegalArgumentException("Vulnerability exception not found with id: $id")
   257→
   258→        vulnerabilityExceptionRepository.deleteById(id)
   259→        log.info("Deleted exception id: {}", id)
   260→    }
   261→
   262→    /**
   263→     * Calculate how many vulnerabilities are affected by an exception
   264→     *
   265→     * Feature 021 Phase 3: Exception impact calculation
   266→     *
   267→     * @param exception The exception to check
   268→     * @return Count of affected vulnerabilities
   269→     */
   270→    fun calculateAffectedCount(exception: VulnerabilityException): Int {
   271→        return when (exception.exceptionType) {
   272→            ExceptionType.IP -> {
   273→                try {
   274→                    vulnerabilityRepository.countByAssetIp(exception.targetValue).toInt()
   275→                } catch (e: Exception) {
   276→                    log.warn("Failed to count IP-based exceptions: {}", e.message)
   277→                    0
   278→                }
   279→            }
   280→            ExceptionType.PRODUCT -> {
   281→                // Count vulnerabilities matching product pattern
   282→                try {
   283→                    vulnerabilityRepository.countByVulnerableProductVersionsContainingIgnoreCase(
   284→                        exception.targetValue
   285→                    ).toInt()
   286→                } catch (e: Exception) {
   287→                    log.warn("Failed to count product-based exceptions: {}", e.message)
   288→                    0
   289→                }
   290→            }
   291→            ExceptionType.ASSET -> {
   292→                // Count vulnerabilities for this specific asset
   293→                if (exception.assetId != null) {
   294→                    try {
   295→                        vulnerabilityRepository.countByAssetId(exception.assetId!!).toInt()
   296→                    } catch (e: Exception) {
   297→                        log.warn("Failed to count asset-based exceptions: {}", e.message)
   298→                        0
   299→                    }
   300→                } else {
   301→                    0
   302→                }
   303→            }
   304→            ExceptionType.CVE -> {
   305→                try {
   306→                    val cveIds = exception.targetValue.split(",").map { it.trim() }.filter { it.isNotEmpty() }
   307→                    if (exception.assetId != null) {
   308→                        vulnerabilityRepository.countByVulnerabilityIdInAndAssetId(cveIds, exception.assetId!!).toInt()
   309→                    } else {
   310→                        vulnerabilityRepository.countByVulnerabilityIdIn(cveIds).toInt()
   311→                    }
   312→                } catch (e: Exception) {
   313→                    log.warn("Failed to count CVE-based exceptions: {}", e.message)
   314→                    0
   315→                }
   316→            }
   317→        }
   318→    }
   319→
   320→    /**
   321→     * Map VulnerabilityException entity to DTO
   322→     *
   323→     * Feature 021: Now includes assetId, assetName, and optionally affectedCount
   324→     * Feature 021 Phase 3: Added affected count calculation
   325→     *
   326→     * @param exception The exception entity
   327→     * @param includeAffectedCount If true, calculate affected vulnerability count
   328→     * @return Exception DTO
   329→     */
   330→    private fun mapToDto(
   331→        exception: VulnerabilityException,
   332→        includeAffectedCount: Boolean = false
   333→    ): VulnerabilityExceptionDto {
   334→        // Fetch asset name if this is an ASSET or CVE exception with assetId
   335→        val assetName = if ((exception.exceptionType == ExceptionType.ASSET || exception.exceptionType == ExceptionType.CVE)
   336→            && exception.assetId != null) {
   337→            assetRepository.findById(exception.assetId!!).orElse(null)?.name
   338→        } else {
   339→            null
   340→        }
   341→
   342→        // Calculate affected count if requested
   343→        val affectedCount = if (includeAffectedCount) {
   344→            calculateAffectedCount(exception)
   345→        } else {
   346→            null
   347→        }
   348→        
   349→        return VulnerabilityExceptionDto(
   350→            id = exception.id,
   351→            exceptionType = exception.exceptionType,
   352→            targetValue = exception.targetValue,
   353→            expirationDate = exception.expirationDate,
   354→            reason = exception.reason,
   355→            createdBy = exception.createdBy,
   356→            createdAt = exception.createdAt,
   357→            updatedAt = exception.updatedAt,
   358→            isActive = exception.isActive(),
   359→            affectedVulnerabilityCount = affectedCount,  // Feature 021 Phase 3
   360→            assetId = exception.assetId,  // Feature 021
   361→            assetName = assetName  // Feature 021
   362→        )
   363→    }
   364→}
   365→

1→package com.secman.service
     2→
     3→import com.secman.domain.*
     4→import com.secman.dto.*
     5→import com.secman.exception.ConcurrentApprovalException
     6→import com.secman.repository.*
     7→import io.micronaut.context.event.ApplicationEventPublisher
     8→import io.micronaut.data.model.Page
     9→import io.micronaut.data.model.Pageable
    10→import jakarta.inject.Inject
    11→import jakarta.inject.Singleton
    12→import jakarta.persistence.OptimisticLockException
    13→import jakarta.transaction.Transactional
    14→import org.slf4j.LoggerFactory
    15→import java.time.LocalDateTime
    16→
    17→/**
    18→ * Core business logic service for vulnerability exception request workflow.
    19→ *
    20→ * Responsibilities:
    21→ * - Create exception requests (with auto-approval for ADMIN/SECCHAMPION)
    22→ * - Approve/reject pending requests (with optimistic locking)
    23→ * - Cancel pending requests
    24→ * - Retrieve requests with pagination and filtering
    25→ * - Calculate summary statistics
    26→ * - Create VulnerabilityException entries on approval
    27→ *
    28→ * Concurrency Control:
    29→ * - Uses optimistic locking (@Version field) to prevent race conditions
    30→ * - First-approver-wins: Multiple simultaneous approvals handled gracefully
    31→ * - Throws ConcurrentApprovalException when optimistic lock fails
    32→ *
    33→ * Feature: 031-vuln-exception-approval
    34→ * Reference: quickstart.md lines 35-91, research.md lines 77-110
    35→ */
    36→@Singleton
    37→open class VulnerabilityExceptionRequestService(
    38→    @Inject private val requestRepository: VulnerabilityExceptionRequestRepository,
    39→    @Inject private val vulnerabilityRepository: VulnerabilityRepository,
    40→    @Inject private val vulnerabilityExceptionRepository: VulnerabilityExceptionRepository,
    41→    @Inject private val userRepository: UserRepository,
    42→    @Inject private val auditService: ExceptionRequestAuditService,
    43→    @Inject private val eventPublisher: ApplicationEventPublisher<ExceptionCountChangedEvent>,
    44→    @Inject private val notificationService: ExceptionRequestNotificationService
    45→) {
    46→    private val logger = LoggerFactory.getLogger(VulnerabilityExceptionRequestService::class.java)
    47→
    48→    /**
    49→     * Create a new exception request.
    50→     *
    51→     * Auto-approval logic:
    52→     * - If requester has ADMIN or SECCHAMPION role → Status = APPROVED, auto-approval flag = true
    53→     * - Otherwise → Status = PENDING (requires manual approval)
    54→     *
    55→     * @param dto Request creation data
    56→     * @param requesterUserId ID of the user creating the request
    57→     * @param clientIp Client IP address for audit logging (optional)
    58→     * @return Created request DTO
    59→     * @throws IllegalArgumentException if vulnerability not found or duplicate active request exists
    60→     */
    61→    @Transactional
    62→    open fun createRequest(
    63→        dto: CreateExceptionRequestDto,
    64→        requesterUserId: Long,
    65→        clientIp: String? = null
    66→    ): VulnerabilityExceptionRequestDto {
    67→        logger.debug("Creating exception request: vulnerabilityId={}, userId={}", dto.vulnerabilityId, requesterUserId)
    68→
    69→        // Validate vulnerability exists
    70→        val vulnerability = vulnerabilityRepository.findById(dto.vulnerabilityId).orElseThrow {
    71→            IllegalArgumentException("Vulnerability with ID ${dto.vulnerabilityId} not found")
    72→        }
    73→
    74→        // Get requester user
    75→        val requester = userRepository.findById(requesterUserId).orElseThrow {
    76→            IllegalArgumentException("User with ID $requesterUserId not found")
    77→        }
    78→
    79→        // Check for duplicate active requests (FR-033)
    80→        checkForDuplicateActiveRequest(dto.vulnerabilityId)
    81→
    82→        // Determine if auto-approval applies
    83→        val isAutoApproved = requester.hasRole(User.Role.ADMIN) || requester.hasRole(User.Role.SECCHAMPION)
    84→        val initialStatus = if (isAutoApproved) ExceptionRequestStatus.APPROVED else ExceptionRequestStatus.PENDING
    85→
    86→        // Create request entity (with sanitized reason to prevent XSS)
    87→        val request = VulnerabilityExceptionRequest(
    88→            vulnerability = vulnerability,
    89→            requestedByUser = requester,
    90→            requestedByUsername = requester.username,
    91→            scope = dto.scope,
    92→            reason = sanitizeInput(dto.reason),
    93→            expirationDate = dto.expirationDate,
    94→            status = initialStatus,
    95→            autoApproved = isAutoApproved
    96→        )
    97→
    98→        // If auto-approved, set review fields immediately
    99→        if (isAutoApproved) {
   100→            request.reviewedByUser = requester
   101→            request.reviewedByUsername = requester.username
   102→            request.reviewDate = LocalDateTime.now()
   103→        }
   104→
   105→        // Save request
   106→        val savedRequest = requestRepository.save(request)
   107→
   108→        // Log creation
   109→        auditService.logRequestCreated(savedRequest, requester, clientIp)
   110→
   111→        // If auto-approved, create exception immediately and log approval
   112→        if (isAutoApproved) {
   113→            createExceptionFromRequest(savedRequest)
   114→            auditService.logApproval(savedRequest, requester, clientIp)
   115→            logger.info("Auto-approved exception request: requestId={}, userId={}", savedRequest.id, requesterUserId)
   116→        } else {
   117→            logger.info("Created pending exception request: requestId={}, userId={}", savedRequest.id, requesterUserId)
   118→            // Publish count change event for real-time badge update (pending count increased)
   119→            publishCountChangeEvent()
   120→
   121→            // Send email notifications to ADMIN/SECCHAMPION users (Phase 10)
   122→            try {
   123→                notificationService.notifyAdminsOfNewRequest(savedRequest)
   124→            } catch (e: Exception) {
   125→                logger.error("Failed to send notification for new request {}: {}", savedRequest.id, e.message)
   126→                // Do not throw - email failures should not block request creation
   127→            }
   128→        }
   129→
   130→        return toDto(savedRequest)
   131→    }
   132→
   133→    /**
   134→     * Approve a pending exception request.
   135→     *
   136→     * Creates corresponding VulnerabilityException on successful approval.
   137→     * Uses optimistic locking to prevent concurrent approvals.
   138→     *
   139→     * @param requestId ID of the request to approve
   140→     * @param reviewerUserId ID of the reviewer (ADMIN or SECCHAMPION)
   141→     * @param reviewDto Optional review comment
   142→     * @param clientIp Client IP address for audit logging (optional)
   143→     * @return Updated request DTO
   144→     * @throws ConcurrentApprovalException if request already reviewed by another user
   145→     * @throws IllegalStateException if request not in PENDING status
   146→     */
   147→    @Transactional
   148→    open fun approveRequest(
   149→        requestId: Long,
   150→        reviewerUserId: Long,
   151→        reviewDto: ReviewExceptionRequestDto?,
   152→        clientIp: String? = null
   153→    ): VulnerabilityExceptionRequestDto {
   154→        logger.debug("Approving exception request: requestId={}, reviewerId={}", requestId, reviewerUserId)
   155→
   156→        val request = requestRepository.findById(requestId).orElseThrow {
   157→            IllegalArgumentException("Exception request with ID $requestId not found")
   158→        }
   159→
   160→        val reviewer = userRepository.findById(reviewerUserId).orElseThrow {
   161→            IllegalArgumentException("User with ID $reviewerUserId not found")
   162→        }
   163→
   164→        // Validate reviewer has ADMIN or SECCHAMPION role (defense-in-depth)
   165→        if (!reviewer.hasRole(User.Role.ADMIN) && !reviewer.hasRole(User.Role.SECCHAMPION)) {
   166→            throw IllegalArgumentException("Only users with ADMIN or SECCHAMPION role can approve exception requests")
   167→        }
   168→
   169→        // Validate status transition
   170→        if (!request.canTransitionTo(ExceptionRequestStatus.APPROVED)) {
   171→            throw IllegalStateException(
   172→                "Cannot approve request in ${request.status} status. Only PENDING requests can be approved."
   173→            )
   174→        }
   175→
   176→        try {
   177→            // Update request status (optimistic lock check happens here)
   178→            request.status = ExceptionRequestStatus.APPROVED
   179→            request.reviewedByUser = reviewer
   180→            request.reviewedByUsername = reviewer.username
   181→            request.reviewDate = LocalDateTime.now()
   182→            request.reviewComment = reviewDto?.reviewComment?.let { sanitizeInput(it) }
   183→
   184→            val updatedRequest = requestRepository.update(request)
   185→
   186→            // Create corresponding VulnerabilityException
   187→            createExceptionFromRequest(updatedRequest)
   188→
   189→            // Log approval
   190→            auditService.logApproval(updatedRequest, reviewer, clientIp)
   191→
   192→            logger.info("Approved exception request: requestId={}, reviewerId={}", requestId, reviewerUserId)
   193→
   194→            // Publish count change event for real-time badge update (pending count decreased)
   195→            publishCountChangeEvent()
   196→
   197→            // Send email notification to requester (Phase 10)
   198→            try {
   199→                notificationService.notifyRequesterOfApproval(updatedRequest)
   200→            } catch (e: Exception) {
   201→                logger.error("Failed to send approval notification for request {}: {}", requestId, e.message)
   202→                // Do not throw - email failures should not block approval workflow
   203→            }
   204→
   205→            return toDto(updatedRequest)
   206→
   207→        } catch (e: OptimisticLockException) {
   208→            // Concurrent modification detected - another user already reviewed this request
   209→            handleOptimisticLockFailure(requestId, "approve")
   210→        }
   211→    }
   212→
   213→    /**
   214→     * Reject a pending exception request.
   215→     *
   216→     * Does NOT create VulnerabilityException.
   217→     * Uses optimistic locking to prevent concurrent reviews.
   218→     *
   219→     * @param requestId ID of the request to reject
   220→     * @param reviewerUserId ID of the reviewer (ADMIN or SECCHAMPION)
   221→     * @param reviewDto Review comment (REQUIRED for rejection, minimum 10 characters)
   222→     * @param clientIp Client IP address for audit logging (optional)
   223→     * @return Updated request DTO
   224→     * @throws ConcurrentApprovalException if request already reviewed by another user
   225→     * @throws IllegalArgumentException if review comment missing or too short
   226→     * @throws IllegalStateException if request not in PENDING status
   227→     */
   228→    @Transactional
   229→    open fun rejectRequest(
   230→        requestId: Long,
   231→        reviewerUserId: Long,
   232→        reviewDto: ReviewExceptionRequestDto,
   233→        clientIp: String? = null
   234→    ): VulnerabilityExceptionRequestDto {
   235→        logger.debug("Rejecting exception request: requestId={}, reviewerId={}", requestId, reviewerUserId)
   236→
   237→        // Validate review comment (required for rejection, minimum 10 characters)
   238→        if (reviewDto.reviewComment.isNullOrBlank()) {
   239→            throw IllegalArgumentException("Review comment is required for rejection")
   240→        }
   241→        if (reviewDto.reviewComment.length < 10) {
   242→            throw IllegalArgumentException("Review comment must be at least 10 characters")
   243→        }
   244→
   245→        val request = requestRepository.findById(requestId).orElseThrow {
   246→            IllegalArgumentException("Exception request with ID $requestId not found")
   247→        }
   248→
   249→        val reviewer = userRepository.findById(reviewerUserId).orElseThrow {
   250→            IllegalArgumentException("User with ID $reviewerUserId not found")
   251→        }
   252→
   253→        // Validate reviewer has ADMIN or SECCHAMPION role (defense-in-depth)
   254→        if (!reviewer.hasRole(User.Role.ADMIN) && !reviewer.hasRole(User.Role.SECCHAMPION)) {
   255→            throw IllegalArgumentException("Only users with ADMIN or SECCHAMPION role can reject exception requests")
   256→        }
   257→
   258→        // Validate status transition
   259→        if (!request.canTransitionTo(ExceptionRequestStatus.REJECTED)) {
   260→            throw IllegalStateException(
   261→                "Cannot reject request in ${request.status} status. Only PENDING requests can be rejected."
   262→            )
   263→        }
   264→
   265→        try {
   266→            // Update request status (optimistic lock check happens here)
   267→            request.status = ExceptionRequestStatus.REJECTED
   268→            request.reviewedByUser = reviewer
   269→            request.reviewedByUsername = reviewer.username
   270→            request.reviewDate = LocalDateTime.now()
   271→            request.reviewComment = sanitizeInput(reviewDto.reviewComment)
   272→
   273→            val updatedRequest = requestRepository.update(request)
   274→
   275→            // Log rejection
   276→            auditService.logRejection(updatedRequest, reviewer, clientIp)
   277→
   278→            logger.info("Rejected exception request: requestId={}, reviewerId={}, comment={}",
   279→                requestId, reviewerUserId, reviewDto.reviewComment?.take(50))
   280→
   281→            // Publish count change event for real-time badge update (pending count decreased)
   282→            publishCountChangeEvent()
   283→
   284→            // Send email notification to requester (Phase 10)
   285→            try {
   286→                notificationService.notifyRequesterOfRejection(updatedRequest)
   287→            } catch (e: Exception) {
   288→                logger.error("Failed to send rejection notification for request {}: {}", requestId, e.message)
   289→                // Do not throw - email failures should not block rejection workflow
   290→            }
   291→
   292→            return toDto(updatedRequest)
   293→
   294→        } catch (e: OptimisticLockException) {
   295→            // Concurrent modification detected
   296→            handleOptimisticLockFailure(requestId, "reject")
   297→        }
   298→    }
   299→
   300→    /**
   301→     * Cancel a pending exception request (requester only).
   302→     *
   303→     * @param requestId ID of the request to cancel
   304→     * @param requesterUserId ID of the user cancelling (must be original requester)
   305→     * @param clientIp Client IP address for audit logging (optional)
   306→     * @throws IllegalArgumentException if user is not the requester
   307→     * @throws IllegalStateException if request not in PENDING status
   308→     */
   309→    @Transactional
   310→    open fun cancelRequest(
   311→        requestId: Long,
   312→        requesterUserId: Long,
   313→        clientIp: String? = null
   314→    ): VulnerabilityExceptionRequestDto {
   315→        logger.debug("Cancelling exception request: requestId={}, userId={}", requestId, requesterUserId)
   316→
   317→        val request = requestRepository.findById(requestId).orElseThrow {
   318→            IllegalArgumentException("Exception request with ID $requestId not found")
   319→        }
   320→
   321→        val user = userRepository.findById(requesterUserId).orElseThrow {
   322→            IllegalArgumentException("User with ID $requesterUserId not found")
   323→        }
   324→
   325→        // Validate ownership
   326→        if (request.requestedByUser?.id != requesterUserId) {
   327→            throw IllegalArgumentException("Only the original requester can cancel this request")
   328→        }
   329→
   330→        // Validate status and auto-approval for APPROVED requests
   331→        if (!request.canTransitionTo(ExceptionRequestStatus.CANCELLED)) {
   332→            throw IllegalStateException(
   333→                "Cannot cancel request in ${request.status} status. Only PENDING requests can be cancelled."
   334→            )
   335→        }
   336→
   337→        // Additional validation for APPROVED requests
   338→        if (request.status == ExceptionRequestStatus.APPROVED && !request.autoApproved) {
   339→            throw IllegalStateException(
   340→                "Cannot cancel manually approved requests. Only auto-approved requests can be revoked by the requester."
   341→            )
   342→        }
   343→
   344→        // If cancelling an auto-approved request, delete the associated VulnerabilityException
   345→        if (request.status == ExceptionRequestStatus.APPROVED && request.autoApproved) {
   346→            deleteExceptionForRequest(request)
   347→            logger.info("Deleted VulnerabilityException for auto-approved request: requestId={}", requestId)
   348→        }
   349→
   350→        // Update status
   351→        request.status = ExceptionRequestStatus.CANCELLED
   352→        val updatedRequest = requestRepository.update(request)
   353→
   354→        // Log cancellation
   355→        auditService.logCancellation(updatedRequest, user, clientIp)
   356→
   357→        logger.info("Cancelled exception request: requestId={}, userId={}, autoApproved={}",
   358→            requestId, requesterUserId, request.autoApproved)
   359→
   360→        // Publish count change event for real-time badge update (only if was PENDING)
   361→        publishCountChangeEvent()
   362→
   363→        return toDto(updatedRequest)
   364→    }
   365→
   366→    /**
   367→     * Get exception request by ID.
   368→     *
   369→     * @param requestId ID of the request
   370→     * @return Request DTO
   371→     */
   372→    fun getRequestById(requestId: Long): VulnerabilityExceptionRequestDto {
   373→        val request = requestRepository.findById(requestId).orElseThrow {
   374→            IllegalArgumentException("Exception request with ID $requestId not found")
   375→        }
   376→        return toDto(request)

   379→    /**
   380→     * Get all exception requests for a specific user (requester).
   381→     *
   382→     * @param userId ID of the requesting user
   383→     * @param status Optional status filter
   384→     * @param pageable Pagination parameters
   385→     * @return Paginated list of user's requests
   386→     */
   387→    fun getUserRequests(
   388→        userId: Long,
   389→        status: ExceptionRequestStatus?,
   390→        pageable: Pageable
   391→    ): Page<VulnerabilityExceptionRequestDto> {
   392→        val requests = if (status != null) {
   393→            requestRepository.findByRequestedByUserIdAndStatus(userId, status, pageable)
   394→        } else {
   395→            requestRepository.findByRequestedByUserId(userId, pageable)
   396→        }
   397→
   398→        return requests.map { toDto(it) }
   399→    }
   400→
   401→    /**
   402→     * Get summary statistics for a user's exception requests.
   403→     *
   404→     * @param userId ID of the user
   405→     * @return Summary statistics DTO
   406→     */
   407→    fun getUserRequestSummary(userId: Long): ExceptionRequestSummaryDto {
   408→        val totalRequests = requestRepository.countByRequestedByUserId(userId)
   409→        val approvedCount = requestRepository.countByRequestedByUserIdAndStatus(userId, ExceptionRequestStatus.APPROVED)
   410→        val pendingCount = requestRepository.countByRequestedByUserIdAndStatus(userId, ExceptionRequestStatus.PENDING)
   411→        val rejectedCount = requestRepository.countByRequestedByUserIdAndStatus(userId, ExceptionRequestStatus.REJECTED)
   412→        val expiredCount = requestRepository.countByRequestedByUserIdAndStatus(userId, ExceptionRequestStatus.EXPIRED)
   413→        val cancelledCount = requestRepository.countByRequestedByUserIdAndStatus(userId, ExceptionRequestStatus.CANCELLED)
   414→
   415→        return ExceptionRequestSummaryDto(
   416→            totalRequests = totalRequests,
   417→            approvedCount = approvedCount,
   418→            pendingCount = pendingCount,
   419→            rejectedCount = rejectedCount,
   420→            expiredCount = expiredCount,
   421→            cancelledCount = cancelledCount
   422→        )
   423→    }
   424→
   425→    /**
   426→     * Get all pending exception requests (for ADMIN/SECCHAMPION approval dashboard).
   427→     *
   428→     * @param pageable Pagination parameters (sorted by oldest first)
   429→     * @return Paginated list of pending requests
   430→     */
   431→    fun getPendingRequests(pageable: Pageable): Page<VulnerabilityExceptionRequestDto> {
   432→        return requestRepository.findByStatus(ExceptionRequestStatus.PENDING, pageable)
   433→            .map { toDto(it) }
